Community comes together at Laurier for 2026 Empty Bowls, raising more than $49,000 to fight food insecurity
TheÂ 28th annual Empty Bowls event raised more than $49,000 in support of The Food Bank of Waterloo Region.
May 29, 2026
Print | PDF
Wilfrid Laurier University welcomed the community to Lazaridis Hall on May 27 for the 28th annual Empty Bowls event, raising more than $49,000 in support of The Food Bank of Waterloo Region.
Guests enjoyed gourmet soups prepared by local chefs and restaurants, served in one‑of‑a‑kind bowls handcrafted and donated by artisans from the Waterloo Potters’ Workshop. Including both lunch and dinner seatings, the event brought together staff, faculty, neighbours and supporters to help fight hunger in Waterloo Region.
“We are grateful for the overwhelming support shown at this year’s Empty Bowls event,” said Suzanne Luke, university art curator of Laurier’s Robert Langen Art Gallery. “The funds raised will make a meaningful difference for individuals and families who rely on The Food Bank of Waterloo Region, and we are proud to help strengthen that support through this long‑standing community partnership.”
Empty Bowls attendees examine handcrafted pottery bowls donated by artisans from the Waterloo Potters’ Workshop.
Empty Bowls is organized by the Robert Langen Art Gallery in partnership with the Waterloo Potters’ Workshop. The event is part of a global movement started by artists and craftspeople who use their work to raise funds for organizations fighting hunger. Globally, Empty Bowls initiatives have generated millions of dollars for food‑related charities.
This year’s event at Laurier continued that legacy. The more than $49,000 raised will provide 98,664 meals for the nearly 73,000 adults and children in Waterloo Region who rely on food assistance. Since launching in 1998, local Empty Bowls events have helped provide more than 1.6 million meals to individuals in Waterloo Region.
This year’s Empty Bowls event was sponsored by Bennett Wealth Management of RBC Dominion Securities Inc., with in‑kind support from Belgian Nursery, Cobs Bread, Canadian Linen, Pottery Supply House and Tucker’s Pottery Supplies.
Learn more about
The Food Bank of Waterloo Region
.
Local dignitaries gather for a photo at the 28th annual Empty Bowls event. Pictured from left to right are The Food Bank of Waterloo Region CEO Kim Wilhelm; City of Waterloo Mayor Dorothy McCabe; Region of Waterloo Councillor Chantal Huinink; City of Waterloo Ward 3 Councillor Hans Roach; Laurier university Librarian Scott Gillies; Becky Riley of The Food Bank of Waterloo Region; and University Art Curator of Laurier’s Robert Langen Art Gallery Suzanne Luke.
Soup is served during the 28th annual Empty Bowls event at Lazaridis Hall on Laurier’s Waterloo campus.

title: Laurier Music therapy alum Madelyn Chung recognized for communications leadership | Wilfrid Laurier University
text:
Skip to main content
Home
Laurier News Hub
Spotlights
Laurier Music therapy alum Madelyn Chung recognized for communications leadership
Madelyn Chung
May 21, 2026
Print | PDF
Wilfrid Laurier University
Master of Music Therapy
alum Madelyn Chung (MMT ’21) has been named communicator of the year by the International Association of Business Communicators (
IABC
) Toronto chapter. Chung was recognized for amplifying marginalized voices and experiences through building the
RepresentASIAN Project
, as well as her dedication to mental wellness as a registered psychotherapist and founder of the
Blossom Mental Health Fund
.
“Madelyn is focused on tackling negative stereotypes and building empathy through her advocacy and storytelling,” says Amie Silverwood, president of IABC/Toronto.
IABC/Toronto’s communicator of the year award celebrates leaders who use communication to inspire change and strengthen communities.
Laurier alum Madelyn Chung was honoured as IABC/Toronto communicator of the year during an event on April 30.
Chung began her career as a journalist, working in fashion and lifestyle media for more than eight years. Although it had been her dream since her teenage years, she became disillusioned with the industry and felt something was missing.
A passion for music was deeply ingrained in Chung by her family. When the classically trained pianist saw a music therapy demonstration, it led her to her next chapter in Laurier’s Master of Music Therapy program.
“Seeing music therapy in action moved me to tears,” says Chung. “I called my mom sobbing and said, this is it. This is what I want to do.”
Laurier’s Master of Music Therapy is currently the only music therapy program in Canada recognized by the College of Registered Psychotherapists of Ontario (CRPO), enabling graduates to work in the regulated field of psychotherapy.
While studying to become a psychotherapist, Chung continued to work as a freelance journalist, pitching stories about her community and her own lived experiences as a Chinese-Canadian woman to media outlets.
“I was writing more from the lens of my own identity,” she says. “A lot of the places that I pitched to said that my perspective was too niche.”
Frustrated by her experience, Chung conceived the RepresentASIAN Project to tell the stories that were important to her but weren’t being told. Launched on March 11, 2020 – the day the World Health Organization declared COVID-19 a worldwide pandemic – the project provided a much-needed outlet during a challenging time.
“It turned out to be such a lifeline of community for the Asian diaspora and for myself to process everything that was happening,” says Chung. “And it’s grown into an incredibly engaged community.”
The RepresentASIAN Project includes interviews with successful creative individuals – including Maggie Kang, the creator of K-Pop Demon Hunters – as well as gift guides featuring Asian-owned businesses. It also features long-form videos, including GENERASIANS, a docuseries that examines Asian Canadian family businesses as they transition to the next generation of owner-operators.
“These are not stories that we typically see in mainstream media, but they are important and valuable nonetheless,” says Chung.
Chung extended the positive impact of the RepresentASIAN Project to tackle a gap in health care, where Asian North Americans are significantly less likely to access mental health services than other segments of the population.
In 2023, she launched the Blossom Mental Health Fund, a registered non-profit focused on supporting the mental health of Asian Canadians by normalizing discussions about mental health through storytelling, removing financial barriers to therapy through grants and providing culturally specific resources.
“Music is such a powerful tool for exploring and expressing your emotions, especially if you have difficulty doing it through words,” she says. “Laurier’s program helped me tremendously in my role as a registered psychotherapist.”
Today, Chung continues her psychotherapy practice, supporting Asian women navigating racial and intergenerational trauma, while growing RepresentASIAN Project as a platform for community storytelling.
“As immigrants or children of immigrants, we often see stories told about us, but not by us,” she says. “The important thing is owning our stories and telling them in ways that are accurate and authentic.”

Laurierâs Lazaridis School of Business and Economics closes the stock market to celebrate 60th anniversary
May 13, 2026
Print | PDF
Kyle Murray, dean of the Lazaridis School of Business and Economics at Wilfrid Laurier University, current students and alumni joined John McKenzie, chief executive officer of TMX Group, to close the Toronto Stock Exchange on May 12 to celebrate the Lazaridis School’s 60th anniversary.
The Lazaridis School of Business and Economics is celebrating its 60th anniversary throughout 2026.
“This anniversary is a moment to celebrate the ideas we’ve sparked, the leaders we’ve developed, and the impact we’ve created together,” says Murray. “As we look ahead, we do so with gratitude for those who built our foundation and excitement for the shared values that will continue to drive our future.”
The Lazaridis School of Business and Economics has more than 6,000 current students in undergraduate and graduate programs and more than 40,000 international alumni.

The MA in Global Justice, Religion and Culture is an exciting new program that offers you a crucial interdisciplinary space for reflection on how religion intersects with these global issues.
Global Studies
Gain the skills you need to tackle global challenges in an increasingly interconnected world. Understand issues impacting communities worldwide and develop strategies for driving change, preparing you for impactful careers in international relations, development, and beyond.
North American Studies (BA in Combination)
Health Sciences
Our Health Sciences program equips you with scientific investigative skills to tackle complex health challenges. Prepare for postgraduate studies in medicine, dentistry, pharmacy, physiotherapy, molecular medical sciences, and public health, enhancing human health and well-being.
Kinesiology (BKin)
Applied Health Sciences (BASc)
The Master of Science (MSc) in Health Sciences is a thesis-based master's program that encompasses two fields of study: Molecular and Medical Sciences (MMS) and Community, Public and Population Health (CPPH). Youâll have the opportunity to choose your focus and conduct research with a faculty supervisor.
Kinesiology (MSc)
Health Studies
Explore how social, biological, cultural and institutional factors affect the health and well-being of communities. This program prepares students to identify significant health challenges in a variety of populations, evaluate whatâs creating risks for individuals and practice how to intervene.
Health Studies + Master of Science, Brighton and Sussex Medical School
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Master of Science (MSc)
Go abroad, gain your master's degree and get ahead in your career. This unique program lets you start your Bachelor of Arts (BA) in Health Studies at Laurier with a conditional offer of admission to the Brighton and Sussex Medical School in the UK.
To grasp our present and future, we must connect with the past, understanding the people, events, and ideas that shaped our reality. This program will enhance your writing, research, and critical thinking skills, contextualizing events and issues historically and culturally.
Indigenous Studies (BA in Combination)
Study History at Laurier to better understand how and why change happens at a global scale. Faculty members will guide your studies and research of both ordinary and exceptional figures from many different locations, from ancient to modern times.
History (BA), Brantford
Our Master of Arts (MA) in History program is part of the Tri-University Graduate Program in History, a partnership of Laurier, the University of Waterloo and the University of Guelph. The integration of history departments makes this program one of the biggest graduate history programs in Canada, while retaining the small classes and supportive professor-student relationship associated with History at Laurier.
Global Justice, Religion and Culture (MA)
Our PhD in History program is part of the Tri-University Graduate Program in History, a partnership of Laurier, the University of Waterloo and the University of Guelph. This collaboration makes it possible for you to access the scholarship and experience of more than 65 graduate faculty and the resources of three universities.
Honours Science
Are you passionate about science, but unsure of what discipline to major in? Perhaps you are interested in pursuing a broad-based science program to support your specific career aspirations. In our Honours Science (BSc) program, you have the freedom to discover your own path in this expansive science-based program.
Science Degree (BSc) + Law Degree (LLB), University of Sussex
Psychology (BA or BSc), Waterloo
Human Relationships
The PhD in Human Relationships builds on your professional experience in the fields of pastoral leadership, and spiritual care and psychotherapy through advanced scholarship and a dissertation. You will be well prepared to make contributions in fields such as ministry, psychotherapy, spiritual care, and higher education.
Human Rights
Study how individuals, organizations and states use human rights to address injustice in Canada and around the world. Our Human Rights program prepares you for careers in human resources, law, social worker, international development, funding and more.
Human Rights + Master's Degree, University of Sussex
Take your passion for justice and meaningful change to the next level, and graduate career-ready, through this five-year program. You'll start your undergraduate Bachelor of Arts in Human Rights at Laurier with a conditional offer of admission to a master's in international development at the University of Sussex in the UK.
Arts Degree (BA) + Master's Degree (MA or MSc), University of Sussex
Indigenous Studies
Indigenous studies is a vibrant field of social, political, historical and cultural study in Canada and around the globe. Laurier's Indigenous Studies program is designed to teach you the perspectives and experiences of Indigenous people and will prepare you to engage with important socio-cultural issues facing Canada and the world.
Integrative Biology
Our Master of Science (MSc) in Integrative Biology provides an interdisciplinary approach to biological research that allows you to explore the answers to complex questions from a perspective that bridges the traditional sub-disciplines of biology, across diverse taxa, over time scales ranging from short (physiological) to long (evolutionary).
Intermediate Economics I and II
Economics provides a foundation for understanding finance, business, and policy. Each certificate lets you learn the fundamentals of economics at your own pace. Together, they give you the key courses required to qualify for a masterâs degree in Economics.
International Education Studies
The first of its kind in Canada, this collaborative program is your pathway to teach in international schools or to pursue education-related work in other fields. Youâll take courses in the Faculty of Education and Department of Global Studies to build global awareness, cultivate intercultural understanding, and learn teaching philosophies and approaches specifically for international settings.
French (BA)
International Public Policy
Are you looking to pursue or enhance a career in international or global policy development? Our Master of International Public Policy (MIPP) gives you the ability to engage with leading scholars and policy practitioners in the field of global governance to follow your passion and turn it into a rewarding career.
Applied Politics (MAP)
Kinesiology
Explore human movement and physical activity through psychology, biomechanics, and physiology. The multidisciplinary approach of our Kinesiology program prepares you for diverse careers in sports, health, and physical therapy by providing a comprehensive understanding of movement and its impact.
Our Master of Science (MSc) in Kinesiology is designed with the objective of providing you with a highly integrated and multidisciplinary approach to the examination of the relationships among physical activity, health and disease in various populations.
Health Sciences (MSc)
Our PhD in Kinesiology provides a highly integrated approach to scholarship, so you can follow your research passion and become an expert in the field. When you complete this program, youâll be able to engage in research and take part in the vital conversation about our nationâs health.
Languages
Achieve fluency in both French and Spanish through Laurierâs Languages program, the first of its kind in Canada. With additional studies in Arabic, German or Italian, you will graduate with the multilingual background that employers are looking for.
Law and Society
Explore the gap between what the law says and what it does with our Law and Society program. Analyze the interactions between law and society, focusing on economics, culture, morality, and politics, while assessing law's role in driving progressive social change.
Leadership
Immerse yourself in Laurierâs comprehensive Leadership program, mastering skills in critical courses like Leadership Foundations, Organizational Leadership, and Influence and Negotiation. This program nurtures future private and public sector heads with strong communication, change management, and ethical decision-making skills.
Management
Our Master of Science (MSc) in Management is a 12-month program where youâll combine coursework and a research project conducted under the guidance and mentorship of our internationally recognized scholars. Choose between one of our three fields: Organizational Behaviour and Human Resource Management; Marketing; or Supply Chain Management.
Located in the Lazaridis School of Business and Economics, our PhD in Management program will turn you into an advanced researcher with both theoretical and practical understanding of the complex problems of business management. We offer five distinct fields: Accounting; Financial Economics; Marketing; Organizational Behaviour and Human Resource Management; Supply Chain, Operations and Technology Management.
Management Analytics
Make smarter business decisions by understanding, analyzing and leveraging your companyâs data with our 12-month professional Master of Science (MSc) in Management Analytics on our Waterloo campus.
Mathematical and Statistical Modelling
Mathematical and statistical modelling is essential a wide range of industries. New types of highly qualified specialists are needed to develop, analyze and apply modern quantitative approaches to a variety of issues of high complexity. Our interdisciplinary PhD program will help you become an independent researcher capable of succeeding in careers in research, industry, teaching, or government.
Mathematics
Study Mathematics at Laurierâs Waterloo campus and develop the analytical and problem-solving skills to tackle real-world challenges. Through a combination of theory, application and technology, the BA and BSc programs offer pathways to careers in finance, research, data science and beyond.
Our Master of Science (MSc) in Mathematics is a unique blend focusing on four areas: financial mathematics, mathematical modelling, statistics and data science, and pure mathematics. Our strength is in preparing students for practical applications and skills applicable in real-life.
Medieval and Medievalism Studies
The Middle Ages are alive and well. Laurierâs Medieval and Medievalism Studies program engages both with the evidence of the Middle Agesâ influence across the globe and with the portrayal of those cultures today in popular entertainment.
Multidisciplinary Studies
Interested in an arts, social sciences, or humanities but unsure which major to declare? The new BA in Multidisciplinary Studies recognizes the individual needs, interests and goals of each student and offers the quality academics of each discipline with unparalleled flexibility to pursue the path that is best for you.
Multidisciplinary Studies + Juris Doctor, Griffith University
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Juris Doctor (JD)
Interested in a career in law, but unsure which major to declare? The flexible Bachelor of Arts (BA) in Multidisciplinary Studies program can be combined with a Juris Doctor (JD) degree from the Griffith University (Queensland, Australia), allowing you to earn your BA and JD in six years.
Music: Collaboration, Curation, and Creative Performance
Embark on a lifelong career in music and the performing arts with our comprehensive and inclusive degree program. Our curriculum embraces diverse musical cultures, instruments, traditions and genres, while balancing collaboration, creation and curation.
Community Music (MA)
Bring your music to life on stage, in the studio, the classroom and the community. Laurierâs Bachelor of Music blends performance, theory, creativity and real-world training so you can shape a degree as unique as your musical path.
Music Therapy (BMT)
Chamber Music (Diploma)
Built around themes of music, health, community and culture, our PhD in Music will benefit students interested in mastering their research skills and looking to make their mark in the fields of music therapy or community music. Internationally unique, the program curriculum is based on a doctoral action plan, problem-based learning, peer learning, and various doctoral special seminar topics.
Music Therapy
Use music to connect, support and heal. Laurierâs Bachelor of Music Therapy is Ontarioâs only undergraduate program of its kind, combining hands-on clinical placements with advanced training in musicianship, psychology and therapeutic techniques.
Our Master of Music Therapy (MMT) is the longest-running graduate music therapy degree in Canada. The philosophy of our program centres around music psychotherapy, clinical musicianship and psychotherapeutic techniques, which are developed through clinical, theoretical and experiential practices.
Navigating the Music Industry Ecosystem
Our online Certificate in Navigating the Music Industry Ecosystem will equip you with a foundational knowledge of royalties, licensing, production, marketing, and more. Through this four-course program, youâll be empowered to monetize your musical skills and succeed in the global music industry.
North American Studies
Explore the ties that bind Canada, the United States, and Mexico together through North American Studies, which examines the region as a distinct historical, cultural, political, and economic entity. This understanding is critical in careers focused on international business, diplomacy, and policy analysis.
Cultural Studies (BA in Combination)
Opera
Step into the spotlight with Laurierâs two-term Opera Diploma. Through full role preparation, performance coaching, and mentorship from leading faculty, youâll bridge the gap between student and professional singer.
Paramedicine
This dynamic degree-completion program is designed for experienced paramedics seeking to advance their careers, explore new opportunities, or enhance their qualifications for future roles. Though it is not a clinical entry-to-practice pathway, this program offers an opportunity for significant professional growth.
Philosophy
Are you curious about the nature of reality, the idea of right and wrong, or the limits of what humans can know? Explore modern concepts of morality, ethics, values, the mind and society.
Policing for Working or Retired Police Officers
Step up in law enforcement with our part-time online Bachelor of Arts in Policing. Tailored for law enforcement professionals with at least one year of experience, this program will prepare you for leadership in your current context or post-policing paths.
Criminology and Policing (BA)
Policing
Designed in consultation with law enforcement agencies across Canada, this program teaches you to examine the critical role and complexities of policing in our modern society. Sharpen your analytical skills in preparation for a career in law enforcement or many other related fields.
Political Science
Society shapes politics and politics shape society. The Political Science program at Laurier will develop your knowledge of the role of government and politics globally, and provide you with skills to contribute to your community and workplace.
Sociology (BA)
Psychology
In Psychology at Laurier Milton, youâll dive into the social science of human behaviour, while benefiting from small classes. Equip yourself with the knowledge of human decision-making and relationships, enabling success in diverse fields such as healthcare, education, community services, and life transitions.
Explore human behaviour and mental processes with a Psychology degree at Laurier Brantford. Through coursework, research, field placements and community service, youâll study topics like cognition, development, neuroscience and forensicsâgaining practical skills and insights for careers in social services, health, education, policy and beyond.
Psychology (BA), Milton
Study Psychology at Laurierâs Waterloo campus and explore topics like mental health, cognition, development and neuroscience. Through lab courses, field placements, research and co-op, youâll gain the academic and applied experience needed for graduate school or careers in health, education or community service.
Psychology and Neuroscience (BSc)
Our Master of Arts (MA) in Psychology faculty members have a wide range of research experience. The program is offered within three fields of study: Community, Developmental and Social Psychology.
Psychology (MSc)
Our Master of Science (MSc) in Psychology program is offered within the cognitive and behavioural neurosciences field. Youâll have the opportunity to start conducting research with a faculty adviser in your first year. Youâll learn how to develop a program of research, use research methodologies and statistical techniques, and communicate your research as an expert in the field.
Psychology (MA)
Our PhD in Psychology program is based on a mentorship model, where faculty members work closely with a small cohort of students. You can choose to specialize in one of four areas of psychology: cognitive and behavioural neurosciences; community psychology; developmental psychology; and social psychology.
Psychology and Neuroscience
Immerse yourself in the major sub-fields of psychology while specializing in neuroscience. You will gain a broad science education with complementary training in other natural sciences, enhancing your expertise in neuroscience.
Public Safety
Revolutionary in both content and delivery, our fully online program delivers the flexibility and practical knowledge needed to succeed in a career in public safety. Examine current challenges facing public safety professionals today and use your classroom learning to make an impact in your community.
Double your degree and knowledge by combining your on-campus studies with our fully asynchronous online Public Safety (BA in Combination) program. Explore the challenges facing public safety professionals and their counterparts, today, while enjoying the flexibility that comes with online learning.
Our fully online graduate diplomas in Public Safety provides you with multidisciplinary competencies that will make you a public safety practitioner with the necessary skill sets to enhance evidence-based decision making and public safety interoperability.
Public Safety (MPS)
Our Master of Public Safety (MPS) is a fully online, part-time professional program for those employed in or seeking employment in the field of public safety. It has purposefully been developed with concentrations that match the four pillars of public safety: national security, border strategies, countering crime and emergency management.
Public Safety (Graduate Diploma)
Religion and Culture
Delve into religious rights, human freedoms, and multicultural coexistence as a Religion and Culture student. Explore ancient and modern spiritual experiences through sacred texts, oral narratives, and art, gaining unique insights that prepare you for diverse and impactful careers.
Our PhD in Religious Studies, with a specialization in Religious Diversity in North America, is offered jointly with the University of Waterloo. Our innovative program has three facets: a North American focus, multidisciplinary theories and methods, and public intelligibility.
Science Degree + Master of Business Administration
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Bachelor of Science (BSc) and Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Begin your studies at Laurier in a Faculty of Science program with a conditional offer to our full-time MBA program after you complete your undergraduate degree. Choose from 6 science programs to combine with a business education.
Science Degree + Law Degree, University of Sussex
Bachelor of Science (BSc) and Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
This Law program provides an international learning experience that is one of the first of its kind for a Canadian university. Earn a Bachelor of Science (BSc) degree from Laurier and a Bachelor of Laws (LLB) degree from the University of Sussex, United Kingdom (UK) in only six years.
Social Justice and Community Engagement
Through an applied focus on a local scale, our Master of Arts (MA) program in Social Justice and Community Engagement (SJCE) is committed to scholarship informed by critical social/environmental justice theories and approaches.
Social Work (MSW)
Professionalize your passion for social justice and community wellbeing. As a social worker, you'll be trained to support individuals, families, and communities facing injustice and harm. Start your journey toward addressing inequality and making a global impact.
Faculty of Social Work
Our Master of Social Work (MSW) curriculum is designed to be at the cutting edge of contemporary social work practice. Our curriculum emphasizes the development of critically reflexive practice skills through the ongoing integration of knowledge, theory and practice within a framework that promotes the advancement of equity, inclusivity, reflexivity and social justice.
Society needs researchers who have a deep understanding of diversity, equity, and inclusion and who are prepared to address inequality and promote social justice. Supported by world-class faculty, students undertake ground-breaking research that fosters social justice and promotes new understandings of social issues affecting individuals, families, organizations, and communities.
Sociology
Get ready for a meaningful career in government, social services, research, and advocacy. This degree explores societal issues related to policy, law, education, and more, equipping you with skills to address and improve these areas.
Women and Gender Studies (BA)
Software Engineering
The new software engineering degree at Laurier emphasizes real-world impact, interdisciplinary education and sustainability by preparing you to tackle urgent global challenges through socially responsible engineering.
Spanish
As the fourth-most spoken language globally, Spanish comprehension is key to communication in business, science, and the arts. Our program helps you to gain fluency in Spanish by applying your skills to creative projects, assignments and international field courses.
Spiritual Care and Psychotherapy
The diploma in Spiritual Care and Psychotherapy will build on your previous education and experience to teach you a unique form of therapy that utilizes spiritual and psychological understandings of healing and growth.
Theology: Spiritual Care and Psychotherapy
A unique form of therapy that integrates spiritual resources and counselling theory into clinical practice, our Master of Arts in Theology: Spiritual Care and Psychotherapy field is recognized by the College of Registered Psychotherapists of Ontario. Graduates of this field of study may be able to pursue professional accreditation with external accrediting bodies once both academic and other criteria required by the accrediting bodies are completed.
Divinity (MDiv)
Theology: Spiritual Care and Psychotherapy and Divinity
Master of Arts (MA) and Master of Divinity (MDiv)
Earn both your Master of Arts (MA) in Theology: Spiritual Care and Psychotherapy and Master of Divinity (MDiv) in three years of full-time study (this double degree can also be completed on a part-time basis). The MA/MDiv double-degree program is valuable if you are preparing for chaplaincy or congregational ministry, and desire a theological foundation and the coursework necessary to apply for accreditation with the College of Registered Psychotherapists of Ontario (CRPO).
User Experience Design
User experience (UX) is a person's experience with a product, service, environment or message. Learn the intellectual tools to think critically about the world and understand how cultural, societal, economic, political and environmental factors contribute to UX.
Game Design and Development (BA)
Laurier's Master of Science (MSc) in User Experience (UX) Design will prepare you to launch your UX career through a focus on practical, professional learning. Learn to design digital services and other products that are user-friendly, intuitive, and easy to navigate as you build your CV and a job-ready portfolio.
Women and Gender Studies
Gender and social justice are concepts central to how humans understand themselves. Learn how gender relates to class, race, ethnicity, nationality, sexuality, age, ability and Indigeneity in a program that places questions of social justice at the centre of inquiry.
Youth and Childrenâs Studies
Our Youth and Children's Studies program empowers you to actively support local youth. Gain research, analytical, and practical skills to effectively engage with and assist todayâs young people, making a tangible impact in your community.

Business Administration (MBA)
Ancient Studies
Coâop
Learn more about the ancient world as you explore the mythology, politics, philosophies, and societies of early civilizations. In this interdisciplinary program, you will discover how global events of ancient times have affected our modern world.
Faculty of Arts
Archaeology and Heritage Studies (BA)
History (BA), Waterloo
Anthropology (BA)
Anthropology
Explore how people in different places and times live, and how they find meaning in their lives. In this program, you will develop an intercultural understanding of how people relate to one another and to the world around them.
Global Studies (BA)
Applied Computing
Waterloo or Brantford
In Person or Online
As technology becomes more sophisticated, so does the skill set required for the computing profession. Laurier's Master of Applied Computing (MAC) will give you an advanced computing degree where you gain theoretical and practical knowledge of mobile computing, enterprise computing, data mining and machine learning.
Faculty of Science
or
Applied Health Sciences
In Person and Online
Medicine is both an art and a science and this program is built around both. Science content is accompanied by social science courses about health, illness and health care, opening up a wide range of prospective employment opportunities.
Faculty of Human and Social Sciences
Health Sciences (BSc)
Health Studies (BA)
Psychology (BA or BSc), Brantford
Applied Politics
The Master of Applied Politics (MAP) is a hands-on graduate program grounded in the discipline of political science. You'll learn real-world skills and gain practical experience in placements with community organizations, local elected officials and government agencies.
International Public Policy (MIPP)
Archaeology and Heritage Studies
Explore past cultures through Laurierâs Archaeology and Heritage Studies program. Reconstruct ancient lifestyles and their impact on todayâs world via field and lab work, gaining hands-on experience in material culture while collaborating with communities to preserve cultural heritage.
Ancient Studies (BA)
Arts Degree + Master of Business Administration
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Begin your undergraduate studies with a conditional offer to our full-time MBA program. Choose a Bachelor of Arts (BA) in Criminology, Game Design and Development, or Leadership at Laurier Brantford then complete your MBA at Laurier Waterloo.
Arts Degree (BA) + Masterâs Degree (MA, MAP or MIPP)
Arts Degree (BA), Waterloo + Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Science Degree (BA or BSc) + Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Start in one of Laurierâs Bachelor of Arts programs with a conditional offer to join our full-time MBA program with co-op upon completing your undergraduate degree. Receive personalized guidance for success in both graduate studies and your career.
Arts Degree (BA), Brantford + Master of Business Administration (MBA)
Arts Degree + Law Degree, University of Sussex
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
Start your journey to a career in law on day one. This program provides the opportunity to earn a Bachelor of Arts (BA) degree from Laurier and a Bachelor of Laws (LLB) degree from the University of Sussex in six years.
Faculty of Liberal Arts
Political Science (BA)
Human Rights (BA)
Arts Degree + Master's Degree, Cardiff University
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Master of Arts (MA) and Master of Science (MSc)
Earn two degrees and invaluable international experience with this new five-year pathway program with Cardiff University. Whether youâre passionate about Communication Studies or Archaeology and Heritage Studies, youâll build career-ready knowledge and skills while studying in both Canada and the United Kingdom.
Arts Degree + Master's Degree, University of Sussex
Graduate with two degrees (from two quality institutions) and a wealth of first-hand international experience. Take your passion for justice, compassion and meaningful change to the next level, and graduate career-ready, through this five-year program.
Human Rights (BA) + Master's Degree (MA or MSc), University of Sussex
Arts Degree (BA) + Law Degree (LLB), University of Sussex
Arts Degree + Masterâs Degree
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Master of Arts (MA) and Master of Applied Politics (MAP) and Master of International Public Policy (MIPP)
Get early acceptance to graduate school with the Bachelor of Arts + Master's degree at Laurier. This is a competitive program for students eager to complete advanced studies in Political Science, History, English, Communication Studies, Film Studies, Global Studies, Languages, Religion and Culture and Women and Gender Studies.
Biochemistry and Biotechnology
Biochemistry and biotechnology are among the fastest-growing fields in science. In this program, you will complete courses in mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology, and biochemistry, equipping yourself with the skills needed to achieve your career goals.
Chemistry (BSc)
Honours Science (BSc)
Biology (BA or BSc)
Biological and Chemical Sciences
Our PhD program in Biological and Chemical Sciences is research-focused, and operates through an apprenticeship model where you will work closely with a faculty supervisor. Your research will fall within one or more of our three areas of research strength: environment and health, biotic interactions, and structure and reactivity.
Biology
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Bachelor of Science (BSc)
Laurier Waterloo offers Biology BA and BSc degree options. Each program provides core biological training, hands-on learning and flexible concentrationsâpreparing you for careers or further studies in science, health, education or public policy.
Environmental Studies (BA)
Business Administration
Learn to adapt to a world of ever-growing complexity and work in teams tackling real-world problems facing todayâs industries and tomorrowâs startups. Our Business of Administration (BBA) program delivers an in-depth, immersive experience revealing the reality of todayâs business world.
Economics (BA)
Communication Studies (BA)
Business Technology Management (BTM)
Business Administration + Computer Science, University of Waterloo
Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) and Bachelor of Computer Science (BCS)
In this double-degree program, you'll earn a Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) from Laurier and a Bachelor of Computer Science (BCS) from UWaterloo in just five years, opening doors to top roles in tech and business.
Business Administration (BBA) + Computer Science (BSc)
Business Administration (BBA)
Computer Science (BA or BSc), Waterloo
Business Administration + Computer Science
Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) and Bachelor of Science (BSc)
We've combined our internationally accredited Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) degree and our Bachelor of Science (BSc) in Computer Science, providing you with the technical know-how to go along with analytical skills and business expertise.
Business Administration (BBA) + Computer Science (BCS), University of Waterloo
Business Administration + Financial Mathematics and Analytics
Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) and Bachelor of Arts (BA)
Earn two desirable degrees in just five years. This accelerated double-degree program gives you a competitive career advantage with a solid understanding of business practices and the complex modern financial instruments used to manage risk and generate profits.
Business Administration (BBA) + Mathematics (BMath), University of Waterloo
Financial Mathematics and Analytics (BA or BSc)
Business Administration + Mathematics, University of Waterloo
Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) and Bachelor of Math (BMath)
Harness the strengths of two top-tier institutions in one program. This is a unique opportunity to master the foundations of business and the applications of math while simultaneously earning two degrees five years.
Business Administration (BBA) + Financial Mathematics and Analytics (BA)
Mathematics (BA or BSc)
Equip yourself with comprehensive business knowledge and practical skills to enhance your career or break into the business field. This part-time diploma covers a wide-range of topics, from accounting and finance, to marketing, management, and micro- and macroeconomics.
Intermediate Economics I and II (Certificates)
Our Business Administration graduate diplomas are a smart way to advance your career. Designed for working professionals, these in-person stackable credentials have the flexibility to let you build your business expertise step-by-step while creating a pathway toward an MBA.
Business Administration (Diploma)
Waterloo or Toronto
Our Lazaridis Master of Business Administration (MBA) is your opportunity to earn a degree that will have a significant impact on your career and future. You'll gain the leadership ability to turn concepts into reality, the interpersonal skills to be a collaborative team player and the insight to make a difference in the world.
Business Economics (MABE)
Finance (MFin)
Management (MSc)
Business Economics

Gender and social justice are concepts central to how humans understand themselves. Learn how gender relates to class, race, ethnicity, nationality, sexuality, age, ability and Indigeneity in a program that places questions of social justice at the centre of inquiry.
Youth and Childrenâs Studies
Our Youth and Children's Studies program empowers you to actively support local youth. Gain research, analytical, and practical skills to effectively engage with and assist todayâs young people, making a tangible impact in your community.

In Person or Online
As technology becomes more sophisticated, so does the skill set required for the computing profession. or

Explore past cultures through Laurierâs Archaeology and Heritage Studies program. Reconstruct ancient lifestyles and their impact on todayâs world via field and lab work, gaining hands-on experience in material culture while collaborating with communities to preserve cultural heritage.

Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Bachelor of Laws (LLB)
Start your journey to a career in law on day one. This program provides the opportunity to earn a Bachelor of Arts (BA) degree from Laurier and a Bachelor of Laws (LLB) degree from the University of Sussex in six years.
Faculty of Liberal Arts
Political Science (BA)
Human Rights (BA)

Earn two degrees and invaluable international experience with this new five-year pathway program with Cardiff University. Whether youâre passionate about Communication Studies or Archaeology and Heritage Studies, youâll build career-ready knowledge and skills while studying in both Canada and the United Kingdom.
Arts Degree + Master's Degree, University of Sussex
Graduate with two degrees (from two quality institutions) and a wealth of first-hand international experience. Take your passion for justice, compassion and meaningful change to the next level, and graduate career-ready, through this five-year program.

Arts Degree (BA) + Law Degree (LLB), University of Sussex

Get early acceptance to graduate school with the Bachelor of Arts + Master's degree at Laurier. This is a competitive program for students eager to complete advanced studies in Political Science, History, English, Communication Studies, Film Studies, Global Studies, Languages, Religion and Culture and Women and Gender Studies.
Biochemistry and Biotechnology
Biochemistry and biotechnology are among the fastest-growing fields in science. In this program, you will complete courses in mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology, and biochemistry, equipping yourself with the skills needed to achieve your career goals.
Chemistry (BSc)
Honours Science (BSc)
Biology (BA or BSc)

Your research will fall within one or more of our three areas of research strength: environment and health, biotic interactions, and structure and reactivity.
Biology
Bachelor of Arts (BA) and Bachelor of Science (BSc)
Laurier Waterloo offers Biology BA and BSc degree options. Each program provides core biological training, hands-on learning and flexible concentrationsâpreparing you for careers or further studies in science, health, education or public policy.
Environmental Studies (BA)

Learn to adapt to a world of ever-growing complexity and work in teams tackling real-world problems facing todayâs industries and tomorrowâs startups. Our Business of Administration (BBA) program delivers an in-depth, immersive experience revealing the reality of todayâs business world.

Communication Studies (BA)
Business Technology Management (BTM)

Business Administration (BBA) + Computer Science (BSc)
Business Administration (BBA)
Computer Science (BA or BSc), Waterloo

Business Administration (BBA) + Computer Science (BCS), University of Waterloo
Business Administration + Financial Mathematics and Analytics

Financial Mathematics and Analytics (BA or BSc)
Business Administration + Mathematics, University of Waterloo

Business Administration (BBA) + Financial Mathematics and Analytics (BA)
Mathematics (BA or BSc)
Equip yourself with comprehensive business knowledge and practical skills to enhance your career or break into the business field. This part-time diploma covers a wide-range of topics, from accounting and finance, to marketing, management, and micro- and macroeconomics.
Intermediate Economics I and II (Certificates)
Our Business Administration graduate diplomas are a smart way to advance your career. Designed for working professionals, these in-person stackable credentials have the flexibility to let you build your business expertise step-by-step while creating a pathway toward an MBA.
Business Administration (Diploma)
Waterloo or Toronto
Our Lazaridis Master of Business Administration (MBA) is your opportunity to earn a degree that will have a significant impact on your career and future. You'll gain the leadership ability to turn concepts into reality, the interpersonal skills to be a collaborative team player and the insight to make a difference in the world.
Business Economics (MABE)
Finance (MFin)
Management (MSc)
Business Economics
Completion of our Master of Arts in Business Economics (MABE) program will put you on track for a career as a professional economist in the private or public sector. Our strength is the merging of business economics with training in economic theory, quantitative methods and applied research.
Management Analytics (MSc)
Finance (MFin) and Management Analytics (MSc) Double Degree
Business Technology Management
Discover a powerful partnership of business and technology education with our Business Technology Management (BTM) program. Enjoy personalized learning in small classes with passionate faculty and dive deep into solving real-world business challenges using digital tools, data analytics and strategic thinking.
Computer Science (BA or BSc), Milton
